page: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-32GB is a general-purpose virtual server hosted on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores, running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 300 GB of local SSD storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

The x86_64 architecture processor includes a cache hierarchy of 64 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 16 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector and neural network operations. Cryptographic tasks are aided by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets.

In benchmark evaluations, the server demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded bzip3 decompression both ranking in the top tier. However, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth metrics, such as large-block read/write speeds, fall into the average tier. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in the PassMark memory score, which ranks in the bottom 10%, indicating potential bottlenecks for memory-intensive operations. LLM inference performance is average for small 135M models but weak for medium 7B models.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and local SSD storage, this server offers a cost-efficient profile for general-purpose workloads that require strong single-thread performance but do not demand extreme memory throughput. It is well-suited for web serving, database operations, and general application hosting.
description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-32GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with an AMD EPYC 9575F processor featuring 8 shared vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 2.0 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 300 GB of local SSD storage. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and hardware-accelerated cryptography. In benchmark testing, the server exhibits top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded decompression speeds, though its multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain average. Notably, its PassMark memory score ranks in the bottom 10%, representing a key performance tradeoff. This server is suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from strong single-thread execution without requiring high-density memory performance.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-32GB is a KVM virtual server with an 8-core AMD EPYC 9575F CPU, 32 GB RAM, and 300 GB SSD, offering top-tier single-core performance alongside average multi-core and memory metrics.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-8xCPU-32GB is an 8-core AMD EPYC virtual server with 32 GB RAM and 300 GB SSD, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced virtual server featuring strong single-core AMD EPYC performance, 32 GB RAM, and local SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 8 shared vCPUs and 8 physical cores
32.0 GB of system memory and 300 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded decompression performance
Average multi-core performance and weak PassMark memory benchmark results
Suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations
